summary: `toml-document` joins the format inventory as an honestly read-only face — the engine reads a member's fields off a TOML document, and no emit round-trip exists

edit.description: `Format` (555-564) carries two members; a third joins, cited to the format it reads. `label` (571-576) and `format_from_label` (975-981) are the closed-vocabulary boundary the doc (551-554) promises — a value outside it stays a load error.

The `json-document` read face's home — `DocumentMember` (105), the whole-artifact-is-one-object read the module doc states (14). The TOML face is the same job over a second format and `toml_edit` is already the sanctioned keystone crate, so this is where the reader is extended or a module beside it is justified; `engineering.md` ("One job, one home") binds the choice, and the commit body names the surface considered and why it did (not) fit. The identity fault `json-document` raises for a kind declaring no `named-field` shape (257-260) is the precedent for the TOML face's own.

`read_file_unit`'s format dispatch (1280-1281) routes a kind's declared format to its adapter; its doc (1262-1268) states why this is the one dispatch — "a per-call-site format match would be a second dispatch to disagree with" — so the TOML face lands here and nowhere else.

The write side must never render a TOML member: 0034 ratifies the face as honestly read-only — no emit round-trip exists for a local member to need. A composed member of a `toml-document` kind refuses loud rather than reaching a writer that is not there (`intent.md`, invariant 6: no path silently degrades). The format-keyed `BodyHasNoHome` refusal (1027-1035) is the shape and the site; the JSON write face (1891-1917) is what this face deliberately has no twin of.
The `Format` union (22) and its doc (19-21) carry the third member — the author's face on the same closed vocabulary.

schemaDelta: none — `format` is a label column on `KindFactRow`; the closed vocabulary it decodes into gains one member.

tests.asserts: a kind declaring `toml-document` reads its member's fields off the TOML document and gates them; a document that is not valid TOML fails loud rather than yielding no fields; a composed member of a `toml-document` kind refuses at emit rather than being written; a format label outside the vocabulary is still refused at load

acceptance: A kind may declare `toml-document` and its members read and gate; nothing writes one (representation.md, "kind": format implementations are engine code, grown by deliberate addition — 0034 ratifies this one). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0034's second derivation — the deliberate-addition path the format-faces asymmetry blesses, `toml_edit` already sanctioned. Read-only is the point, not a stage: DIAL-KIND is the consumer and needs no round trip, and 0034 rejects the local write path outright. Serialized behind the fence re-cut: both edit `src/kind.rs`. scoped at 832f015.

summary: A committed local-locus kind's `governs` declaration overrides discovery's presumptions — gitignore and the workspace skip — for exactly its own scope

edit.description: `discoverable_paths` (347-386) prunes on two presumptions with no local-class awareness: `git_ignore(true)` (355) and the categorical `WORKSPACE_DIR` skip in `filter_entry` (358-365). A real local document is always gitignored, so the shipped class finds members only in fixtures where the file happens to be tracked; and the dial's ratified `.temper/dial.toml` sits under the skipped workspace. Both make rows silently fail to derive — the fail-open invariant 6 forbids. The reviewed `governs` declaration is the authorship claim over its unreviewed documents, so it overrides both, there and only there (0034 errata). `discover_kind_units` (234-241) sees only `governs`; `discover_kind_files` (207-222) already carries the `kind` whose commitment decides. Two fences are NOT the presumptions the errata names and stay whole: `.git/` and the nested-governed-root stop (365-372, `is_governed_root` 388). The comment at 235-238 and `discoverable_paths`'s doc (330-346) state the scoped override rather than the bare presumption.

Install's adoption walk keeps the presumption whole — adoption never converts a local document into a committed member (0034 errata, stated by name). Verify on disk which walk install runs and say in the commit body whether it inherits the carve-out through `import` or is already separate; if it inherits, it is fenced here.
The class's own test home — its scaffold (118) writes fixtures into a temp harness, so the ignore-rule and workspace-skip cases land beside the admissibility and read-time-rows cases they complete.

tests.asserts: a local kind's member is discovered and gated though a `.gitignore` names it, and a local kind governing a path under `.temper/` is discovered though the walk categorically skips the workspace; a committed kind's member under the same ignore rule stays undiscovered; a nested governed root and `.git/` are still fenced from a local kind's walk; install's adoption walk still honors the presumption

acceptance: The declaration is the authorship claim: discovery sees a local-locus kind's per-machine documents despite ignore rules and the workspace skip, there and only there, so `.temper/dial.toml` is reachable (pipeline.md, "Install"). `cargo test` green.
notes: 0034's errata (832f015), the ruling's own post-sweep. Serialized behind the toml face — this entry's workspace-skip fixture is the dial's own `.temper/dial.toml`, which reads under that face — and the linear chain is what puts all three 0034 derivations upstream of DIAL-KIND, which needs every one. scoped at 832f015.

summary: A policy layer is a lock the invocation joins: `check --layer <path>` joins named locks' rows over this corpus, kinds travel by name, and a joined lock failing admissibility fails the check

edit.description: The `Check` command (105-122: `root`, `harness`, `deny_advisories`, `reporter`) gains a repeatable `--layer <path>`. Each named lock's declaration rows join over the host corpus: kinds travel by name, the joined clauses range over the host's selections. Fail-closed costs nothing new — a joined lock that fails admissibility is already a malformed lock, refused loud through the existing `engine::admissibility` path (811/850). 0030's uncommitted rules bind the join: hardening is unbounded in every mode, softening is visible-only and gate-inert.

`read_declarations` (3104) / `parse_declarations` (3127) read one lock today; the join reads the invocation's locks the same way — the lock stays the one interchange, and no second parser is minted beside this one.
Clause rows compose into the host's selections whatever lock carried them; a joined row is an ordinary row.

tests.asserts: `check --layer <lock>` joins the named lock's clauses over the host corpus and gates on them; a joined lock that fails admissibility fails the check loud; a joined clause that would soften the host's gate is inert in block mode while a hardening one binds

acceptance: An org authors an ordinary temper corpus, emits its lock, and distributes it; a consumer joins it at check and is gated by it, fail-closed (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: The third of 0032's five. Org-side authoring stays TS and the lock stays the one interchange — no new format, no trust model, no payload-as-input (0032 rejects that by name: it breaks offline and the payload's internality). Re-gated at 832f015: its blocker CLAUSE-LABEL-IS-AN-ADDRESS shipped (6205707), leaving a dangling tag; now behind 0034's three, sharing `src/main.rs`/`src/drift.rs` with two. scoped f1d97e4; cites re-read 399d8e3.

summary: The `type` predicate accepts a set of lattice kinds — the six documented `string|array(|object)` manifest fields become gateable, and a one-element set is today's behavior exactly

edit.description: `Predicate::Type` (97-102) carries one `kind: ValueType` (101); it becomes a set, satisfied when the field's preserved kind is any member. The variant's doc (93-96) states the widening. `predicate_from_row` (321) decodes the column. An empty set admits nothing and is inadmissible — the inverted-bound precedent `range` and `count` already set.

The `Type` arm (626) matches the feature's preserved kind against one declared kind; it becomes set membership, and the violation message names the declared set. `admissibility` (110) rejects the empty set beside the existing vacuous-clause refusals — the empty `forbidden_keys` case (220) is the shape to follow.

`ClauseRow::value_type` (2672) is `Option<String>` — one lattice name — written at 3511-3512 and read at 3553; it carries the set. An older lock's bare string reads as a one-element set and the next emit rewrites the file whole in canonical form: the robust read plus canonical rewrite an upgraded engine owes a committed lock (`pipeline.md`, "The lock"), never a patch of the file.
The three row-build sites carrying `value_type: None` (226, 253, 275) move with the column.

The `Type` arm (64-67) writes JSON Schema's `type` from one lattice kind via `json_type`; a set is JSON Schema's own type-array, so the `schema` verb keeps saying exactly what the clause says and nothing more.
`Predicate.value_type` (43-47, spelled to match the lock's column) and the `type` ctor (74) take the set; a one-element call stays today's behavior exactly.

`pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s header (606-620) names two holds; the second (615-620, "Wrong-typed component paths") discharges — `skills`, `commands`, `agents`, `hooks`, `mcpServers`, `lspServers` gain `type` clauses over their documented unions, each carrying a fresh raw cite retrieved at encode time, never re-encoded from this description. The first hold (609-614) survives and stays named: `closed-keys` is the widening that closes it.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit; `tests/seam_bindings_current.rs` holds it current.
Generated data, never transcribed — re-derived from the SDK module by the frozen lane (`tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`), which byte-compares it.

The coverage oracle shipped at 97e84a1, after this entry was scoped — it makes this the entry that moves the measured lag, in two places. (1) `covered_rule` (135) destructures `Predicate::Type { field, kind }` and calls `json_schema_type(*kind)` (141): the set breaks that arm, which re-spells as the `type in {a|b}` form `schema_rules` already emits; its doc (132-134) calls a union-typed property "deliberately unreachable here" because a single-kind clause "would reject the documented string form" — that sentence retires with the widening. (2) `EXPECTED_LAG` (34) drops exactly the six rows this closes — `agents`, `commands`, `hooks`, `lspServers`, `mcpServers`, `skills` — and the header's "six union-typed component fields awaiting a `type` that accepts a set" (31-32) retires with them. `monitors`/`outputStyles`/`themes` are union-typed too and stay in the lag: they are components no clause types at all, outside builtins.md's six. `the_lag_behind_the_published_schema_is_the_named_expected_set` (181) fails loud with "re-state EXPECTED_LAG" if this is skipped, so the diff is reviewable by construction, never silent.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow's `value_type` column carries a set of lattice names rather than one; a one-element set is today's behavior, and a bare string an older lock wrote reads as a one-element set.

tests.asserts: a `type` clause over a set holds when the field carries any member kind and fails naming the whole declared set; a one-element set behaves exactly as today's single kind; an empty set is inadmissible; a lock row spelling a bare string reads as a one-element set
the measured lag behind the published schema shrinks by exactly the six union-typed component rules this closes, and the restated `EXPECTED_LAG` is what the oracle now holds
a manifest whose `skills` key carries a documented `string|array` value passes, and one carrying a number fails through the real gate

acceptance: The six documented union-typed manifest fields are gated rather than held (builtins.md, "Default contracts": a hold is named with the widening that closes it, and discharges when it ships). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: First of 0033's four widenings (0029's move). Serialized behind CHECK-JOINS-INVOCATION-LOCKS: both edit `src/drift.rs` and `src/compose.rs`. The wave's four entries each edit `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts` and the generated seam, so they chain rather than fan out. The oracle rider is an audit finding, not a rescope: it shipped after this was scoped and now measures exactly this widening. scoped at 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: Field addressing widens to a declared RFC 9535 subset — name segments and `[*]` only, riding `serde_json_path`; anything beyond the subset is rejected at admissibility

edit.description: `serde_json_path` joins the sanctioned dependency set (0033, human-ruled; re-verify the crate live at encode time). It is hidden mechanics exactly as `regex` (40) hides under `allowed_chars` and `globset` (36) under the governs glob — never a hand-rolled path walker (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "Libraries before hand-rolls"). Two stale-cite riders discharge while the file is open, neither having a carrier of its own: 37-39 sells `regex` as "the `pattern` primitive", a predicate that does not exist (`rg Pattern src/contract.rs src/engine.rs` is empty) and that the corpus refuses outright — its live job is the charset mechanics behind `allowed_chars`; and 42-45 cites `src/schema/interchange.rs` (the module is `src/schema.rs`; no `schema/` dir exists) and assigns ts-rs the interchange-TS role the `sdk/src/generated/` seam bindings superseded (36a7662). Both re-read on disk at 399d8e3.

`FeatureValue` (102-117) flattens both containers: `List` (113) stringifies its elements and `Map` (116) carries no payload at all, so nothing can reach inside either. Extraction retains the nested values the paths evaluate over. 0033 rejects un-flattening `FeatureValue` as the fix **by name** — that would touch the `type` predicate's kind-preservation contract (`kind` at 108, read by the `Type` arm) and every consumer, where path evaluation over retained values needs no row change.

Path evaluation replaces bare-key lookup at the field-reading seam (`features.field(...)`, e.g. the `Type` arm at 626 and `scalar(features, field)` at 708); `[*]` is the each-grain over elements. `admissibility` (110) rejects a path using anything beyond the declared subset — filters, slices, recursive descent — so the RFC engine stays hidden and never surfaces as an author-facing pattern language (`intent.md`, invariant 1; 0033 refuses author-facing JSONPath for the third time on the record).
`field` stays a `String` on both faces — no row delta (0033). The docs on the field-carrying variants state the declared subset and its bound.

`push_unique(&mut required, field)` (63) and `property(&mut properties, field)` (65) treat `field` as a top-level key; `owner.name` is not one. What the `schema` verb says about a nested path is the entry's to settle and it must settle it — spelling a path as a top-level required key would have the editor demand a key the format never documents, a forged finding at the keystroke (`distribution.md`, "The placements and their enforcement modes": validation is the decidable predicates only).
The `field` parameters' docs (`Predicate.field` at 26-27, and the node-scope ctors from 70) state the addressing subset an author may write — names and `[*]`, nothing else.

`marketplaceDefaultContract`'s header (802-818) names one gap behind three bullets. Two discharge: `owner.name` required (806-809) and each `plugins[]` entry's `name`/`source` (810-812) become clauses, with fresh raw cites retrieved at encode time. The third (813-816, the `source` union) **survives and stays named**: it needs a discriminated-union predicate 0033 does not ratify, and the bullet already names that widening, which is what keeps it a hold rather than a silent absence (`builtins.md`, "Default contracts"). The `required("owner")` (866) and `required("plugins")` (872) guidance says the per-entry bar is unreachable; that sentence retires with the reach.
Generated data — re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

schemaDelta: none — `field` is a string on both faces; the subset is a grammar over its contents, checked at admissibility.

tests.asserts: a clause addressing `owner.name` reads the nested value and fires on it; `plugins[*].source` evaluates each-grain over the array's elements; a path spelling a filter, a slice, or recursive descent is inadmissible rather than skipped; a bare name addresses exactly what it addresses today
a catalog whose `owner` object omits `name`, and one whose `plugins[]` entry omits `source`, each fail through the real gate

acceptance: `owner.name` and `plugins[*].source` are spellable and gated; the RFC engine is hidden mechanics and a path beyond the declared subset is refused (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's second widening. Its human half rides but is not this entry's to write: CLAUDE.md's stack list is a `.temper` projection, so adding `serde_json_path` there is a `chore(harness)` act (CLAUDE.md, "The two harnesses") — name it in the commit body rather than editing the projection. Serialized behind TYPE-ACCEPTS-A-SET over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped at 15b7a3a.

summary: `closed-keys`: a clause declaring the kind's already-declared `required`/`optional` key set exhaustive — an undeclared key is a finding, and `plugin-manifest`'s `--strict` bar becomes spellable

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (81) gains `ClosedKeys` — a variant naming no field of its own: it consumes the kind's already-declared `Required` (83-88) and `Optional` (89-94) rows as the allow-list, so the key set is never authored twice (0033). `predicate_from_row` (321) decodes it.

Evaluation reads the contract's own sibling rows rather than one field's value — a predicate needing wider context is evaluation cost, never a category (`contract.md`, "clause": the degree/reachability precedent). `Predicate::Optional` returns `Outcome::Holds` (621) and nothing consumes the record today; `closed-keys` is what consumes it. `admissibility` (110) rejects a `closed-keys` clause on a kind declaring no keys — vacuous, the shape the empty `forbidden_keys` refusal (220) already sets. The `ForbiddenKeys` arm (695) is the deny-list complement this does not replace.
`ClauseRow` (2627) needs no argument column for it — the allow-list derives from the kind's sibling rows, so the row is predicate/severity/guidance/cite alone.
`clause_from_row` (176-190) lifts it; the row-build sites carry no new column.

The exhaustive `match` (62) needs an arm, and `closed-keys` is the one predicate whose JSON Schema face is the whole object's rather than a property's: `additionalProperties: false`. The no-op arm list (110-120) is where a predicate lands when it has no schema face; this one has one.
A `closedKeys()` ctor joins the node-scope predicates (70-80); it names no field, and its doc says the allow-list is the kind's own declared keys, never a second list.

`pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s first hold (609-614) — unrecognized top-level fields, "the substance of `--strict`" — discharges: the manifest's documented keys already ride as `required`/`optional` rows, and one `closedKeys()` clause declares them exhaustive, with a fresh raw cite retrieved at encode time. The header's parenthetical that nothing consumes `optional` (612-613) retires with it. The shipped `forbiddenKeys` deny-list slice ("the one deny-list slice that *is* decidable") stays unless it is now subsumed — check on disk and say which in the commit body (`engineering.md`, "One job, one home").
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit.
Generated data — re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (135) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so a new variant is a compile error until it lands in an arm; `ClosedKeys` joins the no-op list (147-165), whose comment already reasons about the `--strict` bar. `EXPECTED_LAG` must NOT move, and that is pinned rather than asserted here: the shipped `the_published_schema_states_no_closed_key_rule_so_the_strict_bar_is_outside_this_diff` (217) reads the vendored schema's absent `additionalProperties` and states this widening "closes that hold against the *docs*, and this oracle will not move when it lands". If the lag test fires anyway, the arm is wrong, not the list.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow carries a `closed-keys` predicate key with no new argument column — the allow-list is the kind's own `required`/`optional` rows.

tests.asserts: a member carrying a key the kind declares neither required nor optional is a finding at the clause's declared severity; a member carrying only declared keys holds; the clause on a kind declaring no keys is inadmissible; the allow-list is read from the kind's rows, so adding an `optional` row admits the key with no second edit
a manifest carrying an undocumented top-level key fails through the real gate — the `--strict` bar the kind's contract mirrors

acceptance: `plugin-manifest`'s contract checks the strictest documented profile it claims — `claude plugin validate --strict` — rather than naming the gap as a hold (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's third widening. `optional` has been a row nothing reads since it shipped; this is the consumer that makes it load-bearing, so the first `closed-keys` clause is also the first real test that a kind's declared rows are complete. Serialized behind FIELD-ADDRESSING-RFC-9535-SUBSET over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped at 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: `shape`: a predicate over a closed enum of engine-implemented, doc-cited shapes, opening with the two documented ones — the skill name's hyphen rules and no-XML-tags-in-description

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (81) gains `Shape { field, shape }` over a new closed `Shape` enum whose members are engine-implemented and doc-cited, opening with exactly two: the skill name's hyphen rules (no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen) and no-XML-tags-in-description. An unknown shape is rejected at load exactly as an unknown predicate is, never skipped (`contract.md`, "clause": the vocabulary is closed and growing it is a deliberate language change) — `predicate_from_row` (321) is that boundary. No author ever writes a pattern: 0033 refuses the author-facing pattern clause for the third time on the record.

Each shape's mechanics are `regex`, hidden exactly as it hides under `allowed_chars` (708). The violation message names the shape and the clause's guidance teaches it. `admissibility` (110) needs no new refusal — an unknown shape never decodes into a clause at all.

`ClauseRow` (2627) gains a `shape` column carrying the enum member's name, following the `value_type` precedent (2672, written at 3511-3512 and read at 3553): the row family carries the argument as its name and decodes it at the boundary rather than carrying an engine type.
`clause_from_row` (176-190) and the three row-build sites (226, 253, 275) carry the column.

The exhaustive `match` (62) needs an arm, and which one is a real choice the entry must make and state: emitting a shape's hidden regex as JSON Schema's `pattern` puts a pattern in front of an author through the back door, while the no-op arm list (110-120) leaves the keystroke quieter than the gate. `distribution.md` ("The placements and their enforcement modes") bounds it — the generated schema is the decidable predicates only, and it is machine output, never clause surface.
A `shape()` ctor and a `Shape` string-union type join the node-scope predicates (70-80): an author picks a named shape and can spell nothing else — the closed enum is the type.

`skillDefaultContract`'s header (889-894) names both holds and already names this widening ("a narrow shape predicate governs additions"); both discharge — the name's hyphen rules and the platform's "no XML tags in the description" become clauses with fresh raw cites retrieved at encode time. The `allowedChars` name clause (914) already keeps XML out of the *name* and its guidance says so; this is the description's own bar, and the two must not double-cover the name.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit.
Generated data — re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (135) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so `Shape` is a compile error until it lands in an arm. It belongs in the no-op list (147-165) — the oracle measures the plugin-manifest floor against the published schema, this widening ships clauses on `skill` alone, and the schema states no shape rule for a `type`/`minLength`-vocabulary diff to count. `EXPECTED_LAG` must not move; if it does, the arm is wrong.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow gains a `shape` column carrying the declared shape's enum name, when the predicate is `shape`.

tests.asserts: a skill name with a leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen is a finding and a clean slug is not; a description carrying an XML tag is a finding; a lock row naming a shape the enum does not carry is refused at load rather than skipped; no clause surface accepts an author-written pattern

acceptance: The two documented decidable spec rules the skill contract has named as a hold since it shipped are gated, over a closed enum no author can extend (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's fourth widening — the "later wave under their own design" 0022 reserved. The enum's closure is the whole safety property: it is what separates a shape from the open lint `intent.md` invariant 2 exists to prevent, so a shape lands with its doc cite or it does not land. Serialized behind CLOSED-KEYS-CLAUSE over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped at 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: Ship the temper-owned `dial` kind: a local-locus document naming clauses by label and declaring the severity this machine reads them at — deletion unspellable, softening inert in block mode

edit.description: `all_kinds` (374-388) ships eleven claude-code kinds; the dial joins as temper's own — its schema IS 0030's envelope: an entry names a clause by its compiled label and declares a severity, and the schema cannot spell deletion, so a dialed clause still reports.

The provider face. The dial is **temper-owned, not a claude-code kind**, so it exports from the root, never the `claude-code` subpath (`specs/builtins.md`: the shipped claude-code kinds export from that subpath alone).
The root export carries the dial.

The dial's document face is ruled (0034): a local-locus `toml-document`. Both halves are upstream code by the time this is picked — `Commitment::Local` (46-56) and `CustomKind::local` (730) shipped at 40619a0, LOCAL-LOCUS-IS-READ-SIDE-ONLY retires the `local_locus_fault` layout-only arm (746) that would otherwise refuse this kind on contact, and TOML-DOCUMENT-READ-FACE puts `toml-document` in `Format` (555-564). This entry declares the kind; it mints no format and no locus mechanics of its own.
Check applies the dial's severities to the labelled clauses it names, and its softening is inert in block mode — so a block-mode pass on any machine implies the shared gate's pass.

schemaDelta: none of the lock's own — the dial is a shipped kind declaring a local locus over the `toml-document` format, and a local member's rows never enter the lock, deriving at read time instead.

tests.asserts: a dial entry re-reads a clause at the declared severity and the clause still reports; a dial cannot spell deletion; dialed softening is inert in block mode while dialed hardening binds in every mode

acceptance: See the annoying finding, read its label, dial it — locally, uncommitted, visibly, and without ever softening the shared gate (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Un-forked at 832f015: 0034 ruled `(local-locus-toml-face)` whole — the dial stays local-locus TOML, and "layout-only" retires as the wrong fence rather than the dial bending to it. `dependsOnForks` cleared; the record is deleted from open-questions. Now serialized only, behind SHAPE-PREDICATE over `sdk/src/builtins.ts`, with all three 0034 derivations upstream through the chain. scoped f1d97e4; re-read 399d8e3.

summary: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock — the gate says out loud which inputs judged this run

edit.description: The check run announces the three uncommitted-or-joined inputs by name: each active local member, each dialed clause (by its label), each joined lock. The announcement is unconditional — a run judged by inputs review never saw says so, whatever the verdict.

Every reporter renders one diagnostic source (`specs/distribution.md`): `session_start` (65), `github` (141), `sarif` (172) each carry the announcement in their own shape rather than one reporter growing a private line.

tests.asserts: a check run with a local member, a dialed clause, and a joined lock announces all three; a run with none announces nothing extra

acceptance: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: Last of 0032's five — it announces what the other four introduce, so it is honest only once they exist. Serialized behind the dial (shares `src/main.rs`) and inherits its fork through the chain. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. Every cite below re-read on disk at a94cfb5 — 6d145fa moved them all ~6 lines up, so the numbers here are the current ones, not the scoped ones. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (621) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (648), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 519 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 618-620 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 643-646 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1357, re-read at a94cfb5) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1358-1362) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1360) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at a94cfb5 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. The prior 'every cite stands unmoved' claim is retired as false: 6d145fa moved all of them ~6 lines (constant 65→59, narration 525→519 and 624→618, BFS 654→648, test 1356→1357), each re-read on disk and re-stamped above. The constant is still 5, so the park's subject is untouched — and may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified a94cfb5.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 399d8e3, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 9409a6c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped at b676656.
